project.name: 内容喵 AI
project.description: AI 电商短视频/图片生成平台，核心为智能工作流引擎

layers.name: API 路由层
layers.description: Fastify HTTP 路由处理器，覆盖项目管理、视频生成、脚本编写、素材库、广场等全部业务端点的请求解析与响应格式化

layers.name: 业务模块层
layers.description: 核心业务逻辑模块，包含视频制作各步骤（服饰搭配、角色定妆、脚本分镜、视频生成）、热点趋势、裂变视频、逆向脚本等领域的编排与服务

layers.name: 基础设施服务层
layers.description: LLM 调用、媒体处理、对象存储、异步任务队列、技能加载等跨领域基础设施服务，定时调度器、数据持久化辅助、适配器及后端工具函数

layers.name: 数据访问与类型契约层
layers.description: PostgreSQL 数据访问层与类型契约，按驱动表组织 Repository 封装 SQL，DTO 类型定义和 Repository 端口契约保障编译期类型安全

layers.name: 后端入口层
layers.description: Fastify 应用初始化、路由注册、服务绑定等启动流程，以及应用主入口、服务器配置和零散业务策略文件

layers.name: 前端界面层
layers.description: React 页面组件（按视频/图片/换装/逆向脚本项目流程和管理后台组织）、共享 UI 组件、布局组件、自定义 Hooks 和前端工具函数

layers.name: 前端核心层
layers.description: 后端 API 客户端封装、WebGL/GPU 视频渲染管线、转场特效引擎、媒体处理库等前端基础设施与业务模块

layers.name: 抖音发布扩展
layers.description: Chrome 浏览器扩展，自动化抖音创作者平台的视频发布流程，包括 Cookie 管理、表单填写、封面上传等操作

layers.name: 提示词管理系统
layers.description: Skills 提示词模板体系，包含各业务场景（服饰分析、脚本生成、角色生成等）的 System/User Prompt 模板、共享规则与参数 Schema

layers.name: 项目支撑层
layers.description: 部署脚本与 Nginx 配置、SQL 建表脚本、项目规划文档、OpenSpec 变更规格、README、以及构建和运行时配置文件

tour.title: Project Overview & Entry Point
tour.description: The README documents the core concept: an AI e-commerce short video and image generation platform built around a multi-step workflow engine. Video projects follow a 6-step pipeline (outfit styling, character setup, script+storyboard, video generation, delivery, fission), while image projects follow 4 steps. The server.ts entry point boots a Fastify 5 HTTP server, and app.ts wires together the entire application through the app-setup orchestration layer. Start here to understand what this system does and how it starts up.

tour.title: Application Bootstrap Layer
tour.description: The app-setup directory is the control center for application initialization. setup-core.ts binds infrastructure services (database pool, storage, LLM clients) into the Fastify context. setup-routes.ts (545 lines) registers every HTTP route module -- over 100 route files covering all business endpoints from Step 1 through Step 6. setup-executors.ts (2276 lines) wires up async job executors for long-running AI tasks like video generation, script creation, and image processing. This layered startup pattern ensures every dependency is available before any route handles a request.
tour.languageLesson: Fastify uses a plugin-based architecture where setup functions receive the Fastify instance and register routes, decorators, and hooks. The app-setup layer leverages Fastify's dependency injection to make services like database pools and LLM clients available to all route handlers through the request context.

tour.title: Route Layer -- Step-by-Step API
tour.description: Routes follow the project's workflow steps literally: step1-outfit handles garment upload and classification, step2-character manages role portrait and five-view generation, step3-candidate covers script generation and storyboard creation, and step4-video orchestrates AI video clip generation and merging. Each route directory contains an index.ts that registers HTTP handlers. The route layer enforces a strict rule: no SQL here, no business logic -- only HTTP parsing, authentication, calling module/repo layers, and response formatting. Individual handlers are capped at 15 lines.

tour.title: Business Modules -- Script & Storyboard Engine
tour.description: The business module layer is where the AI workflow logic lives. step3-script-orchestrator.ts coordinates the multi-stage script generation pipeline: parsing input, analyzing hotspots, creating scripts, quality checking, and formatting output. Under video-step/step3, each script strategy (emotion archetype, fashion, custom scenario, product showcase, library-based) is a self-contained module with its own concept generator, prompt builder, and type definitions. The emotion-archetype module is particularly rich -- it includes an archetype library, selector, outline generator, storyboard generator, and validator. This modular design allows new script strategies to be added without modifying existing ones.

tour.title: LLM & AI Service Integration
tour.description: All AI calls flow through a unified LLM transport layer. llm-transport.ts abstracts multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) behind a consistent interface, routing requests based on provider configuration and call mode. The skill-loader.ts service loads prompt templates from the Skills system at runtime with 50ms caching, replacing the old database-based approach. This means every LLM call uses a version-controlled, Git-managed prompt template -- no hardcoded prompts anywhere in the codebase. Provider route keys determine which model and API format to use for each specific task.
tour.languageLesson: The provider route key pattern decouples business logic from AI provider specifics. Each route key maps to a specific model, API endpoint, and call format (streaming vs batch, chat completions vs images API). This lets the system switch providers or models by changing configuration rather than code.

tour.title: Data Access & Type Contracts
tour.description: The data layer enforces strict separation: repositories in src/repositories/pg/ encapsulate all SQL behind a per-table pattern where each nrm_* database table has its own repository file extending a base class. The contracts directory defines TypeScript interfaces and DTO types that guarantee compile-time type safety between the route, module, and repository layers. This three-tier separation (Route -> Module/Service -> Repository) ensures SQL never leaks into business logic and business logic never touches HTTP parsing.
tour.languageLesson: The Repository pattern here uses PostgreSQL parameterized queries through a connection pool, with each repository owning all SQL for its driving table -- including JOINs and aggregates. The base repository class provides common CRUD operations, while specialized repositories add domain-specific queries.

tour.title: Frontend Architecture
tour.description: The React 19 frontend starts from App.tsx which mounts the Layout component. Layout.tsx is the navigation hub: it renders the sidebar, the workflow stepper (showing which project step the user is on), and manages project type routing (video, image, outfit-change, reverse). The backendApi.ts service layer mirrors the backend route structure with typed API client modules (step1, step2, step3, step4, projects, reverse, square, etc.), all built on a centralized request handler with authentication tokens. SSE (Server-Sent Events) hooks provide real-time progress updates for long-running AI tasks.

tour.title: Video Project Flow Pages
tour.description: The video project pages implement the 6-step workflow as separate workspace components. Step3WorkspaceRoute.tsx is the script+storyboard workspace where users review AI-generated scripts, edit storyboard frames, and trigger regeneration. Step4VideoWorkspaceScreen.tsx is the video generation workspace with controls for clip preview, transition effects, music selection, cover image, and beat synchronization. Each workspace is a full-screen experience with its own state management, real-time progress tracking, and specialized editors. The workspace pattern separates each step into an isolated UI module that communicates with the backend through the step-specific API services.

tour.title: Image Project Flow Pages
tour.description: Image projects follow a parallel but distinct 4-step workflow. ImageProjectLayout.tsx provides the shared layout with step navigation for image-specific flows. The most complex page is ImageEcommerceEditor.tsx (Step 4), which is a drag-and-drop e-commerce detail page editor where users compose product showcases with model photos, text overlays, and layout templates. It features a real-time phone preview, section tree management, version history, and SVG graphics overlay -- essentially a mini design tool built in React for generating product listing pages from AI-created imagery.

tour.title: Skills Prompt Management System
tour.description: The Skills system is a Git-native prompt management architecture that replaced database-stored prompts. Each skill is a directory under skills/ containing SKILL.md (metadata), system.md or .hbs (system prompt template), user.md or .hbs (user prompt template), schema.ts (input parameters), and optional shared rules. The system supports 50+ skills covering every AI scenario: outfit analysis, five-view character generation, script strategies (emotion archetype, fashion, product showcase, custom), storyboard generation, video step analysis, and quality scoring. Handlebars templates enable conditional rendering and variable injection. The skill-loader.ts service loads these from disk with caching, making prompts version-controllable, reviewable through pull requests, and instantly deployable without database changes.
tour.languageLesson: Handlebars (.hbs) templates support {{variable}} interpolation, {{#if condition}} blocks, {{#each items}} loops, and {{{tripleStash}}} for unescaped HTML output. The triple-stash pattern is used for injecting shared rule fragments that contain markdown formatting.

tour.title: Douyin Publisher Chrome Extension
tour.description: The Chrome extension automates video publishing to Douyin (TikTok China). The background/index.ts entry point coordinates a task-runner that handles cookie management, API communication with the main backend, and anti-detection measures. The content scripts inject into the Douyin Creator platform to automate form filling, video upload, cover selection, and publish actions. This extension is the delivery bridge for Step 5 of the video workflow -- it takes the final generated video and publishes it directly to the creator's Douyin account without manual intervention.

tour.title: Deployment & Project Configuration
tour.description: The deployment infrastructure uses shell scripts in .deploy/ for production deployment and service restarts. The root package.json defines the monorepo workspace structure with npm as the package manager (yarn is explicitly forbidden). The project uses a monorepo layout where the backend lives at the root (src/) and the frontend is under apps/web/, with separate build, dev, and test commands for each. Environment configuration flows through .env files, with database connections, LLM API keys, and storage credentials managed as environment variables rather than hardcoded values.
